title: Idempotent Consumers, Inbox Records, and External Effects · Type Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs idempotent consumers, inbox records, and external effects from bounded publish, route, delivery, acknowledgement, and recovery evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the durable inbox and duplicate-safe effect protocol. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for durable inbox and duplicate-safe effect protocol after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata.
Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible.
Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review messaging team handles case 2040 in a benefit-notification consumer near its database pool limit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata.
